title: More Nonsense Limerick 59
content:
There was an old man of El Hums,
    Who lived upon nothing but crumbs,
    Which he picked off the ground, 
    With the other birds round,
    In the roads and the lanes of El Hums.
meaning:
"More Nonsense Limerick 59" is a quintessential example of Edward Lear's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
